manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: penguin, blueberry, milkshake, hummingbird, mask, eyelash, pastry, hubcap, cranberry.
Your opponent's team words are: fishhooks, crate, cello, lollipop, underwear, anaconda, vodka, jar.
Distractor words are: fern, bug, undershirt, egg, motorboat, doughnut, pillow.
The kill words are: octopus.

message.reasoning:
We must produce a single-word clue and list target words it relates to. Must not use any words from any lists or parts of them. Ensure clue not same as any listed words. Provide clue that relates to multiple team words. Team words: penguin, blueberry, milkshake, hummingbird, mask, eyelash, pastry, hubhub? actually hubcap, cranberry. Want clue that links many. Perhaps "bird" relates to penguin and hummingbird. Also "berry" relates to blueberry and cranberry. Could combine? Can't have two separate clues; we give one clue word only. Could choose "fruit" relates blueberry, cranberry; maybe also pastry? Not really. Could choose "sweet" relates blueberry, pastry, milkshake? But also could be ambiguous with opponent's lollipop. Might be okay. Need to list target words we think clue connects to. Must be safe not overlapping with opponent or distractors. "Dessert" could relate blueberry (pie), milkshake, pastry, cranberry? maybe.

Better to choose clue "bird" linking penguin and hummingbird and maybe mask? No. Could include "eyelash" not. Let's think of clue "tropical" maybe hummingbird, blueberry? Not.

Maybe "cream" links milkshake, pastry, mask? Not.

What about "feather"? penguin (has feathers), hummingbird (feathers), maybe eyelash (like tiny feathers). Could be three. That's decent.

Check if "feather" is in any list? No.

So we can target: penguin, hummingbird, eyelash. Provide clue.

But need think opponent words that might be associated with "feather". Opponent: fishhooks, crate, cello, lollipop, underwear, anaconda, vodka, jar. None obviously. Distractors: fern, bug, undershirt, egg, motorboat, doughnut, pillow. Egg could be related to feather (bird). That's a distractor, risk. Might cause opponent guess? The partner may guess incorrectly maybe.

Maybe choose clue "bird" linking penguin and hummingbird, but also could be mistaken with anaconda (snake) not bird. That seems safe. No distractor "bird". Good.

Clue "bird": targets penguin, hummingbird. Also maybe mask? No.

Better than three.

Maybe clue "berry" linking blueberry and cranberry. That's two. But "berry" could also be related to "bug" (distractor) maybe? Not directly. Opponent's "lollipop" maybe sugary. But safe.

Could we combine more? Maybe clue "sweet" linking blueberry, milkshake, pastry, cranberry (cranberry sauce). That's many. But also could be linked to lollipop (opponent) and doughnut (distractor) and maybe jar (jam). That risks.

Goal to maximize correct guesses quickly but safe. Probably best to give two-word clue? Only single word allowed. Choose "bird" for two words. Or "berry" for two words. Let's choose "berry" to get blueberry and cranberry, also maybe pastry (berry pastry?). Might be okay but partner might guess only blueberry, cranberry. Provide targets list accordingly: blueberry, cranberry.

Clue "berry" not in any list. Works.

Thus answer:

CLUE: berry
TARGETS: blueberry, cranberry

Check that "berry" is not part of any words. No.

Return format
